issue_title: Warhammer® 40,000: Dawn of War® - Dark Crusade (4580) not running on Fedora
compatibility_report:

- Steam AppID of the game: 4580 

## System Information
- GPU: 
-   Vendor: Intel (0x8086)
    Device: Mesa Intel(R) HD Graphics 610 (KBL GT1) (0x5906)

compatibility_report:
# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues: Virtual Fighter Maneuvers Playtest
- Steam AppID of the game: 4192060

## System Information
- GPU: AMD Radeon RX 7800 XT
- Video driver version: Mesa 25.2.7
- Kernel version: 6.17.9
- Link to full system information report as [Gist](https://gist.github.com/): I can share if needed.
- Proton version: 10.0-3

## I confirm:
- [x] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [ ] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

<!-- Please add `PROTON_LOG=1 %command%` to the game's launch options and
attach the generated $HOME/steam-$APPID.log to this issue report as a file.
(Proton logs compress well if needed.)-->

## Symptoms <!-- What's the problem? -->

When running Proton 10.0-3, the left eye renders fine, but the right eye seems to be in the wrong position and potentially distorted. I am using a Valve Index.

I can attach a Proton log if needed.

## Reproduction

1. Launch game (must be in the playtest) with Proton 10.0-3.
2. After the game starts rendering (in the loading screen, in the main menu, and when playing), right eye position and distortion issues should be visible.

<!--
1. You can find the Steam AppID in the URL of the shop page of the game.
   e.g. for `The Witcher 3: Wild Hunt` the AppID is `292030`.
2. You can find your driver and Linux version, as well as your graphics
   processor's name in the system information report of Steam.
3. You can retrieve a full system information report by clicking
   `Help` > `System Information` in the Steam client on your machine.
4. Please copy it to your clipboard by pressing `Ctrl+A` and then `Ctrl+C`.
   Then paste it in a [Gist](https://gist.github.com/) and post the link in
   this issue.
5. Also, please copy the contents of `Help` > `Steam Runtime Diagnostics` to
   the gist.
6. Please search for open issues and pull requests by the name of the game and
   find out whether they are relevant and should be referenced above.
-->

## Workaround

Switch to Proton Experimental (as of right now, should be the 2025-11-26 version). Afterwards it appears to work fine.
symptom_summary:
用户在使用Proton 10.0-3运行《Virtual Fighter Maneuvers Playtest》时，右眼的渲染位置错误且可能存在失真现象，而左眼渲染正常。 

Tags: Visual Glitches, Distortion, Rendering Issues

issue_title: Dark Souls: Remastered unable to use Dualsense controller
compatibility_report:
# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues: Dark Souls: Remastered
- Steam AppID of the game: 570940

## System Information
- GPU: GTX 1080 TI
- Driver/LLVM version: Nvidia 470.94
- Kernel version: 5.15.11
- Link to full system information report as [Gist](https://gist.github.com/Lodger/de82a6fb900eca30154922d590e2f96a):
- Proton version: 6.3-8

## I confirm:
- [X] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [X] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

<!-- Please add `PROTON_LOG=1 %command%` to the game's launch options and
attach the generated $HOME/steam-$APPID.log to this issue report as a file.
(Proton logs compress well if needed.)-->

## Symptoms <!-- What's the problem? -->
I'm unable to use a Dualsense controller with Dark Souls: Remastered. These are my current controller settings: 
![shot-2022-01-12_13-43-11](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/24976475/149202158-3745dbda-9be2-41ad-8b8f-a1cde807b5c5.jpg)

The game behaves simply as if no controller is plugged in; no input from the controller affects the game at all, except for the touch pad, because that also controls the mouse. Portal 2 works flawlessly with the same settings (although it doesn't use Proton), so I'm certain the controller works. Enabling Steam Input in the properties tab does not fix the problem, nor does big picture mode. I haven't tested this with any other controllers because I don't have any.

## Reproduction
As far as I know, just try to use a Dualsense controller with Dark Souls: Remastered.

<!--
1. You can find the Steam AppID in the URL of the shop page of the game.
   e.g. for `The Witcher 3: Wild Hunt` the AppID is `292030`.
2. You can find your driver and Linux version, as well as your graphics
   processor's name in the system information report of Steam.
3. You can retrieve a full system information report by clicking
   `Help` > `System Information` in the Steam client on your machine.
4. Please copy it to your clipboard by pressing `Ctrl+A` and then `Ctrl+C`.
   Then paste it in a [Gist](https://gist.github.com/) and post the link in
   this issue.
5. Please search for open issues and pull requests by the name of the game and
   find out whether they are relevant and should be referenced above.
-->
symptom_summary:
用户在《黑暗之魂：重制版》中无法使用Dualsense控制器，游戏似乎没有检测到控制器的输入，除了触控板可以控制鼠标外，其他输入完全无效。启用Steam输入和大屏幕模式也未能解决此问题。

Tags: Controller Issues, Input Not Detected, Steam Input

compatibility_report:
# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues: `Monster Hunter Rise`
- Steam AppID of the game: `1446780`

## System Information
- GPU: `RX 7900 XTX`
- Driver/LLVM version: `4.6 Mesa 22.3.3; LLVM 15.0.6-r1`
- Kernel version: `6.1.4-zen2-znver3-o3-pds`
- Link to full system information report as [Gist](https://gist.github.com/): https://gist.github.com/kzdixon/89c2e8be835357675ede275c054d9dc5
- Proton version: `Proton Experimental` and/or `Proton 7.0`

## I confirm:
- [x] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [x] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

<!-- Please add `PROTON_LOG=1 %command%` to the game's launch options and
attach the generated $HOME/steam-$APPID.log to this issue report as a file.
(Proton logs compress well if needed.)-->

## Symptoms <!-- What's the problem? -->

Monster Hunter Rise never fully compiles shaders on RX 7900 XTX.

## Reproduction

1. Launch Monster Hunter Rise
2. Watch as it "compiles" shaders
3. See that it never finishes even if left open for hours

<!--
1. You can find the Steam AppID in the URL of the shop page of the game.
   e.g. for `The Witcher 3: Wild Hunt` the AppID is `292030`.
7. You can find your driver and Linux version, as well as your graphics
   processor's name in the system information report of Steam.
8. You can retrieve a full system information report by clicking
   `Help` > `System Information` in the Steam client on your machine.
9. Please copy it to your clipboard by pressing `Ctrl+A` and then `Ctrl+C`.
   Then paste it in a [Gist](https://gist.github.com/) and post the link in
   this issue.
10. Please search for open issues and pull requests by the name of the game and
   find out whether they are relevant and should be referenced above.
-->

Tested on all versions of Proton I usually use: Proton 7.0-5/6, Proton Experimental (Bleeding-Edge) and GE-Proton7-43.

Update: This is fixed a recent `mesa` merge (see https://gitlab.freedesktop.org/mesa/mesa/-/merge_requests/20941). Should be released in the near future I assume.

I believe the fix has been merged in and released as of `mesa 22.3.5`.

symptom_summary:
用户在使用Proton 7.0-6e或更高版本时，游戏《LunarLux》在发布者logo后崩溃，较低版本可以进入开始画面。Steam Overlay的使用非常卡顿，并且NumLock键在Overlay中无法使用，偶尔在尝试使用Overlay时也会崩溃。

**Tag Suggestions:** Crashes, Lag, Overlay Issues, Input Problems
